story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create QueryFilesDto with validation (AC: 4.5.1, 4.5.2, 4.5.3, 4.5.4) - Subtask 1.1: Create src/modules/files/dto/request/query-files.dto.ts - Subtask 1.2: Add @IsOptional() page (default: 1, min: 1, type: number) - Subtask 1.3: Add @IsOptional() limit (default: 20, min: 1, max: 100, type: number) - Subtask 1.4: Add @IsOptional() @IsString() mimeType (filter by MIME type) - Subtask 1.5: Add @IsOptional() @IsString() search (search in originalName) - Subtask 1.6: Add @IsOptional() @IsString() sortBy (default: 'createdAt') - Subtask 1.7: Add @IsOptional() @IsEnum(['asc', 'desc']) sortOrder (default: 'desc') - Subtask 1.8: Apply @Type(() => Number) to page and limit for proper parsing
- Task 2: Add GET /files endpoint to FilesController (AC: 4.5.1) - Subtask 2.1: Create listFiles() method in FilesController - Subtask 2.2: Apply guard: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) (NO PermissionsGuard) - Subtask 2.3: Use @Query(ValidationPipe) queryDto: QueryFilesDto - Subtask 2.4: Extract @CurrentUser() user (domainID, userID, permissions) - Subtask 2.5: Call FilesService.listFiles(queryDto, domainID, userID, hasViewAllPermission) - Subtask 2.6: Return paginated response with data and meta
- Task 3: Implement listFiles() in FilesService (AC: 4.5.2, 4.5.3, 4.5.4, 4.5.5) - Subtask 3.1: Create listFiles() method signature - Subtask 3.2: Build base filters: domainID, deletedAt === null - Subtask 3.3: Add userID filter if NOT admin (hasViewAllPermission === false) - Subtask 3.4: Add mimeType filter if provided (support wildcards) - Subtask 3.5: Add search filter if provided (originalName LIKE) - Subtask 3.6: Validate sortBy field (allowed: createdAt, filename, size, mimeType) - Subtask 3.7: Build orderBy clause - Subtask 3.8: Calculate skip for pagination - Subtask 3.9: Query files with Prisma findMany - Subtask 3.10: Query total count with Prisma count - Subtask 3.11: Calculate totalPages - Subtask 3.12: Return data and meta
- Task 4: Add pagination meta type (AC: 4.5.6) - Task 5: Error handling (AC: 4.5.4) - Task 6: Testing (AC: All) - 17 unit/E2E tests

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
1. **AC-4.5.1:** GET /files endpoint created - Route: GET /api/files - Guards: JwtAuthGuard (NO permission guard) - Query parameters: page, limit, mimeType, search, sortBy, sortOrder - Returns paginated FileResDto[]
2. **AC-4.5.2:** Pagination implemented - Default: page=1, limit=20 - Max limit: 100 items per page - Response includes meta: { page, limit, total, totalPages } - Uses Prisma skip/take for pagination
3. **AC-4.5.3:** Filtering implemented - mimeType filter: Supports wildcards (e.g., "image/*", "application/pdf") - search filter: Searches in originalName (case-insensitive LIKE) - Filters combined with AND logic - All filters optional
4. **AC-4.5.4:** Sorting implemented - Default: sortBy='createdAt', sortOrder='desc' (newest first) - Supported sort fields: createdAt, filename, size, mimeType - Sort order: 'asc' or 'desc' - Invalid sort fields rejected (400 Bad Request)
5. **AC-4.5.5:** Access control implemented - Regular user: Only sees own files (WHERE userID = currentUserID) - Admin with FILES.VIEW_ALL: Sees all files in domain (no userID filter) - Always filtered by domainID (WHERE domainID = currentUserDomainID) - Always excludes soft-deleted files (WHERE deletedAt IS NULL)
6. **AC-4.5.6:** List response validated - Response: { success: true, data: FileResDto[], meta: { page, limit, total, totalPages } } - FileResDto[] is array of file metadata - meta.total is count of all matching files (not just current page) - meta.totalPages calculated correctly: Math.ceil(total / limit)
7. **AC-4.5.7:** Performance validated - Query uses indexes (domainID, userID, mimeType, deletedAt) - Response time < 150ms (p95) for 20 items - Pagination prevents loading entire table - No N+1 queries

artifact.title: Epic Technical Specification: File Management (AWS S3)
artifact.snippet: Complete AC specifications (AC-4.5.1 through AC-4.5.7), pagination using Prisma skip/take pattern, filtering with wildcard support for mimeType, sorting with field validation, access control logic (regular users see own files, admin with FILES.VIEW_ALL sees all domain files), performance targets (response time < 150ms p95).

artifact.title: Epic Technical Specification: File Management (AWS S3)
artifact.snippet: QueryFilesDto specification with validation decorators: @IsOptional() page (default: 1), limit (default: 20, max: 100), mimeType (string filter), search (string), sortBy (default: 'createdAt'), sortOrder (enum: 'asc' | 'desc'). Uses @Type(() => Number) for numeric fields.

artifact.title: Epic Technical Specification: File Management (AWS S3)
artifact.section: APIs and Interfaces - File List Endpoint
artifact.snippet: GET /files endpoint specification: JwtAuthGuard (NO PermissionsGuard), query parameters for pagination/filtering/sorting, response format with data and meta (page, limit, total, totalPages), access control logic based on FILES.VIEW_ALL permission.

artifact.title: Epic Technical Specification: File Management (AWS S3)
artifact.snippet: File list queries use pagination (skip/take), max 100 items per page. Database indexes on File entity: [domainID], [userID], [mimeType], [deletedAt]. Compound index consideration: [domainID, userID, deletedAt] for user file list queries. Response time target: < 150ms (p95) for 20 items.

artifact.title: Story 4.4: File Deletion (Soft Delete)
artifact.section: AC-4.4.6 - Soft-deleted files excluded from list endpoint
artifact.snippet: GET /files must exclude files with deletedAt !== null. Admin with FILES.VIEW_ALL also doesn't see soft-deleted files. Soft-delete filter pattern established: WHERE deletedAt IS NULL must be applied to all query operations.

artifact.reason: Add new GET /files endpoint with listFiles() method. Existing structure has upload, download, delete endpoints established. Apply @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) (NO PermissionsGuard for list).

Implement listFiles(queryDto: QueryFilesDto, domainID: string, userID: string, hasViewAllPermission: boolean) method. Build filters, handle pagination, sorting, access control. Return { data: File[], meta: PaginationMeta }.

Reuse for list response (array of FileResDto). Already excludes sensitive fields (s3Key, s3Bucket, domainID, userID, deletedAt). Includes: id, filename, originalName, mimeType, size, sizeFormatted, createdAt.

File entity with indexes on [domainID], [userID], [mimeType], [deletedAt]. Query performance optimized for filtering and sorting. deletedAt field used for soft-delete filtering (WHERE deletedAt IS NULL).
Apply to GET /files endpoint for authentication. Extract user from JWT with @CurrentUser decorator.
Extract user (domainID, userID, permissions) from JWT in listFiles() controller method.
Check if user has FILES.VIEW_ALL permission to determine if they can see all domain files (admin) or only own files (regular user).
Permission constant for admin access to view all domain files. Used in access control logic for listFiles().
Add E2E tests for GET /files endpoint. Test pagination, filtering, sorting, access control, domain isolation, soft-delete exclusion.

story-context.constraints:
- **Pagination Pattern:** Standard pagination using Prisma skip/take. Default: page=1, limit=20. Max limit: 100 (prevent performance issues). Meta includes: page, limit, total, totalPages.
- **Filtering Strategy:** mimeType with wildcard support ("image/*" → Prisma: `mimeType: { startsWith: 'image/' }`). search filter: case-insensitive LIKE on originalName (Prisma: `originalName: { contains: search, mode: 'insensitive' }`). All filters optional, combined with AND logic.
- **Sorting Mechanism:** Allowed sort fields: createdAt, filename, size, mimeType (validated to prevent SQL injection). Default: sortBy='createdAt', sortOrder='desc'. Invalid sort field → 400 Bad Request.
- **Access Control Logic:** Regular user sees only own files (WHERE userID = currentUserID). Admin with FILES.VIEW_ALL sees all domain files (no userID filter). Domain isolation always enforced (WHERE domainID = currentUserDomainID). Soft-delete filtering always enforced (WHERE deletedAt IS NULL). NO PermissionsGuard on endpoint (custom logic in service based on FILES.VIEW_ALL).
- **Performance Optimization:** Database indexes on [domainID], [userID], [mimeType], [deletedAt]. Compound index consideration: [domainID, userID, deletedAt] for user file list queries. Pagination prevents full table scans (skip/take limits query size). Response time target: < 150ms (p95) for 20 items.
- **Soft-Delete Pattern Compliance:** All queries MUST include deletedAt === null filter. Soft-deleted files treated as non-existent (excluded from list). Even admin with FILES.VIEW_ALL cannot see soft-deleted files (no restore feature in MVP).
- **Error Handling:** Invalid sortBy field → BadRequestException('Invalid sort field'). Invalid page/limit values → ValidationPipe handles (400 Bad Request). No files found → Return empty array with total: 0 (not an error).

interface.name: QueryFilesDto
interface.signature: class QueryFilesDto { page?: number; limit?: number; mimeType?: string; search?: string; sortBy?: string; sortOrder?: 'asc' | 'desc'; }

interface.name: FilesService.listFiles
interface.signature: listFiles(queryDto: QueryFilesDto, domainID: string, userID: string, hasViewAllPermission: boolean): Promise<{ data: File[], meta: { page: number, limit: number, total: number, totalPages: number } }>

interface.name: FileResDto
interface.signature: class FileResDto { id: string; filename: string; originalName: string; mimeType: string; size: number; sizeFormatted: string; createdAt: Date; }

interface.name: PaginationMeta
interface.signature: interface PaginationMeta { page: number; limit: number; total: number; totalPages: number; }

tests.standards: Testing follows NestJS testing standards using Jest. Unit tests for FilesService.listFiles() with mocked PrismaService and AuthorizationService. E2E tests for GET /files endpoint using Supertest with real database (test DB or in-memory). Test coverage targets: 90%+ for services, 80%+ for controllers. All tests must verify pagination, filtering, sorting, access control, domain isolation, and soft-delete exclusion.
tests.ideas:
**Unit Tests (FilesService.listFiles()):** - AC-4.5.5: Regular user → only own files returned (userID filter applied) - AC-4.5.5: Admin with FILES.VIEW_ALL → all domain files returned (no userID filter) - AC-4.5.2: Pagination (page=2, limit=5) → correct skip (5) and take (5) - AC-4.5.3: Filter by mimeType ('image/*') → only images returned - AC-4.5.3: Filter by search ('document') → only files matching originalName - AC-4.5.4: Sorting (sortBy='size', sortOrder='asc') → files sorted by size ascending - AC-4.5.5: Soft-deleted files excluded → files with deletedAt !== null not in results - AC-4.5.4: Invalid sortBy field → throws BadRequestException - AC-4.5.2: No files → returns empty array with total: 0 - AC-4.5.6: Meta calculation → totalPages = Math.ceil(total / limit)
**E2E Tests:** - AC-4.5.1: GET /files (regular user) → only own files returned - AC-4.5.5: GET /files (admin with FILES.VIEW_ALL) → all domain files returned - AC-4.5.2: Pagination (page=1, limit=10) → 10 items, meta correct - AC-4.5.3: Filter by mimeType ('application/pdf') → only PDFs - AC-4.5.3: Filter by search ('test') → matching files - AC-4.5.4: Sorting (sortBy='createdAt', sortOrder='desc') → newest first - AC-4.5.5: Domain isolation → user from domainA cannot see domainB files - AC-4.5.5: Soft-deleted files excluded → upload → delete → list → not in results - AC-4.5.1: Unauthorized (no JWT) → 401 Unauthorized
